questions.question: What is an arithmetic progression?
questions.correct_answer: A sequence in which the difference between consecutive terms is constant
questions.explanation: The constant difference is called common difference.

questions.question: What is the common difference of an AP?
questions.correct_answer: The difference between any term and its preceding term

questions.question: What is the formula for the nth term of an AP?
questions.explanation: It gives the general term of the AP.

questions.question: What type of AP has d > 0?
What type of AP has d < 0?
What type of AP has d = 0?
Can an AP be finite or infinite?
questions.correct_answer: An AP can be finite or infinite
questions.explanation: It depends on whether the sequence ends.

questions.question: What does the graph of an AP represent?
questions.explanation: AP terms lie on a linear graph.

questions.question: Which real-life situation follows an AP?
questions.explanation: Increase is by a constant amount.

questions.question: What is the first term of an AP denoted by?

notes.title: Definition of AP
notes.content: AP is defined by a constant difference between consecutive terms.

notes.title: Types of AP
notes.content: AP can be increasing, decreasing or constant.

notes.title: nth Term Formula
notes.content: The nth term formula helps find any term of an AP.

notes.title: Graph of AP
notes.content: Graph of an AP is linear in nature.

notes.title: Applications of AP
notes.content: APs are used to model patterns with fixed change.

facts.fact: AP is based on constant difference, not ratio.
If d = 0, all terms are equal.
AP is a type of numerical sequence.
nth term formula is used frequently in exams.
